input.situation: SaaS startup with 500 users, $10K MRR, considering whether to focus on enterprise sales or product-led growth

situation_analysis.current_state: Early traction with 500 users at $20 ARPU, indicating market fit but not yet at scale
key_factors: Small team limits simultaneous strategy execution
Runway creates urgency for revenue growth

options.name: Product-Led Growth
options.approach: Double down on self-serve funnel: improve onboarding, add viral features, optimize conversion.

options.name: Enterprise Outbound
options.approach: Hire 1 AE, target 10-50 enterprise accounts with custom pricing.

options.name: Hybrid: PLG + Outbound Assist
options.approach: Keep self-serve as base, add light outbound for top 10% prospects.

trade_offs.analysis: Enterprise is faster per-deal but PLG scales better long-term
PLG is cheaper but enterprise gives revenue predictability
Single strategy learns faster but carries more risk

recommendation.rationale: With 3 engineers and limited runway, PLG maximizes the team's strength (product) while avoiding the cost and risk of enterprise sales. The existing 500-user base provides a foundation to optimize.
recommendation.revisit_if: If self-serve conversion doesn't improve 2x within 3 months, pivot to hybrid.

risks.risk: PLG growth too slow for runway
risks.mitigation: Set 3-month checkpoint, pre-identify 5 enterprise targets as fallback
A/B test all onboarding changes, maintain current flow as control

next_steps.action: Build and launch first onboarding improvement
